creator: bsd-unix@embarqmail.com
text:
First:  This PR does not show up at in Portsmon:

http://portsmon.freebsd.org/portoverview.py?category=ports-mgmt&portname=pkg&wildcard=

Is this something I did wrong?

Second: I'm unsure if this is actually a pkg updating problem or a
ports/UPDATING entry problem.  Specifically this entry:

  20131216:
    AFFECTS: users of databases/db4*
    AUTHOR: mandree@FreeBSD.org

I have db42-4.2.52_5 installed but pkg updating does not show this
entry.  If I change the UPDATING entry to a single line entry of:

AFFECTS: users of databases/db4* /databases/db41* databases/db42*
databases/db43* databases/db44* databases/db46* databases/db47*
databases/42o*

This is based on a current ports tree:

databases/db4/           databases/db42-nocrypto/ databases/db47/
databases/db41/          databases/db43/          databases/db48/
databases/db41-nocrypto/ databases/db44/          databases/db4o-mono/
databases/db42/          databases/db46/

Then pkg updating works for this entry.  I am not sure how much can be
wildcarded in this entry.  Perhaps it could be condensed.  I looked at
the porters handbook (10.2) and there are no guidelines for the use of
wildcards.

I looked at pkg/updating.c and it seemed to be looking for the origins
to determine if it should be displayed.  I could be wrong on this very
easily since I'm not very proficient in C.

If this is an UPDATING entry problem then this PR can be closed but
at least the 20131216 should be fixed.  Perhaps a clarification of
this in the Porters Handbook would help if the AFFECTS entry is the
problem.

As people switch to PKGNG and start to rely on pkg updating it could
create user problems if its not reliable.

Randy
